task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select Osimertinib as the cancer drug
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drug 'Osimertinib' is typed into the search bar or selected in the cancer drugs list within the app.
The medication 'Propranolol' is typed into the search bar or selected in the co-medications list within the app.
A result screen is displayed showing the interaction between Osimertinib and Propranolol, including a traffic-light color code (e.g., Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, or Grey).
A terminal or text editor is visible showing a command or text saving the identified color name (e.g., 'green') to the file /sdcard/answer.txt.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app GUI to find the interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through the Liverpool Cancer iChart app rather than using a web browser or terminal to look up the interaction.

integrity.description: The agent checked the interaction in the app before writing the answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction result screen in the app appears in the screenshots prior to the answer being written to the text file.

integrity.description: The agent wrote only the color name to the answer file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text written to /sdcard/answer.txt contains only the color name (e.g., 'green') without additional conversational text or explanation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Osimertinib is a cancer drug and Propranolol is a co-medication. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app uses a traffic light color system: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, and Grey/Gray. The agent must find the interaction color between these two drugs in the app and write ONLY the color name to /sdcard/answer.txt.
